mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month rise in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances following a UNRATE increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields a negative or flat 60-day forward return in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with a rolling Rank IC below 0.02 over any 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market absorption, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 60-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP exceeds 2.0 percent, confirming sustained economic expansion that boosts corporate earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0 percent fails to predict positive 20-day returns during mid-cycle expansions, with a hit rate below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sudden supply-side contraction and recession risk, prompting immediate portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.0 percent month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances following an INDPRO drop greater than 1.0 percent.
